title: Kfz-Daten – Österreich (Neuzulassungen & Bestand)
text: Das Paket enthält vier Funktionen für österreichische Kfz-Daten, aufgeteilt Neuzulassungen und Bestand. Alle Funktionen entdecken URLs dynamisch — kein manuelles Link-Update nötig, wenn Statistik Austria neue Dateien veröffentlicht.

title: Kfz-Daten – Österreich (Neuzulassungen & Bestand)
text: Gibt den Kfz-Bestand (alle angemeldeten Fahrzeuge zum Stichtag) zurück. Der type-Parameter steuert, welche Dimension zurückgegeben wird.

title: Kfz-Daten – Österreich (Neuzulassungen & Bestand)
text: Datenquellen: - Jahreswerte (31. Dezember): 2019–2025. Enthält alle Kraftfahrzeuge nach Bundesland sowie Anhänger-Gesamtzahl für Österreich. - Vorläufige Daten (aktuellstes Quartal): z. B. 31. März 2026. Enthält zusätzlich Anhänger aufgeschlüsselt nach Bundesland. Spalten: stichtag, vorlaeufig, section ("Kraftfahrzeuge" / "Anhänger"), bundesland, fahrzeugart, anzahl
code: # Alle Jahreswerte 2019–2025 + aktuellster vorläufiger Wert statistik_kfz_bestand() # Nur Jahreswerte, keine vorläufigen Daten statistik_kfz_bestand(include_vorlaeufig = FALSE) # PKW-Bestand nach Bundesland über die Jahre statistik_kfz_bestand() |>   filter(section == "Kraftfahrzeuge",          fahrzeugart == "Personenkraftwagen",          bundesland != "Österreich") |>   select(stichtag, bundesland, anzahl) |>   arrange(bundesland, stichtag) # Anhänger-Bestand für Österreich statistik_kfz_bestand() |>   filter(section == "Anhänger", bundesland == "Österreich") |>   select(stichtag, fahrzeugart, anzahl)

title: Kfz-Daten – Österreich (Neuzulassungen & Bestand)
text: Datenquellen: - tab_3 aus den Jahres-ODS (2019–2023): PKW-× Kraftstoffart × Bundesland, direkt aus dem Haupt-ODS jedes Jahres. - Separate Kraftstoffart-Dateien (2024+): Kfz-BestandKraftfahrzeugBundeslandKraftstoffartEnergiequelle{year}.ods — alle Fahrzeugtypen × Kraftstoffart × Bundesland (reichhaltigerer Datensatz). - Vorläufige Daten: Pkw_nach_Kraftstoff-Sheet — PKW-× Kraftstoffart × Bundesland. Spalten: stichtag, vorlaeufig, bundesland, fahrzeugart, kraftstoffart, anzahl Hinweis: Für 2019–2023 ist fahrzeugart immer "Personenkraftwagen" (PKW-). Ab 2024 enthält der Datensatz auch alle anderen Fahrzeugtypen (Lkw, Motorräder usw.). Der vorläufige Datensatz hat fahrzeugart = "Personenkraftwagen Klasse M1".
code: # PKW/Kfz nach Kraftstoffart — 2024-12-31, 2025-12-31, vorläufig 2026 statistik_kfz_bestand(type = "kraftstoff") # Elektro-PKW nach Bundesland, letzter verfügbarer Stichtag statistik_kfz_bestand(type = "kraftstoff") |>   filter(fahrzeugart == "Personenkraftwagen Klasse M1",          kraftstoffart == "Elektro") |>   arrange(desc(stichtag), desc(anzahl)) # Alle Kraftstoffarten für PKW in Österreich gesamt über die Zeit statistik_kfz_bestand(type = "kraftstoff") |>   filter(fahrzeugart == "Personenkraftwagen Klasse M1",          bundesland == "Österreich") |>   select(stichtag, kraftstoffart, anzahl) |>   arrange(stichtag, kraftstoffart)

title: Kfz-Daten – Österreich (Neuzulassungen & Bestand)
text: Jahreswerte (alle Bundesländer): Personenkraftwagen, Krafträder, Motorfahrräder, Lkw Klasse N1, Lkw Klasse N2+N3, Sattelzugfahrzeuge, Zugmaschinen, Sonstige Kfz. Vorläufige Daten (granularer): Personenkraftwagen Kl. M1, Motorräder Kl. L3e, Lkw N1/N2/N3, Omnibusse, Wohnmobile, Zugmaschinen, Anhänger Kl. O und R, Wohnanhänger, u. v. m.

title: Kfz-Daten – Österreich (Neuzulassungen & Bestand)
text: Der wichtigste Datensatz für Trendanalysen bei Neuzulassungen. Gibt monatliche PKW-Neuzulassungen nach Bundesland und Kraftstoffart von Jänner 2006 bis zum aktuellsten verfügbaren Monat zurück. 2006–2025: kommt aus dem gebündelten Dataset at_pkw_kraftstoff_hist (offline, sofort verfügbar) Aktuelles Jahr: wird live von der Statistik-Austria-Website geladen Spalten: bundesland, kraftstoffart, year, month, date, anzahl
code: # Elektro-Zeitreihe für ganz Österreich statistik_pkw_kraftstoff_zeitreihe(kraftstoffart = "Elektro") # Wien: Elektro vs. Diesel seit 2020 statistik_pkw_kraftstoff_zeitreihe(   date_from     = "2020-01",   bundesland    = c("Wien", "Österreich"),   kraftstoffart = c("Elektro", "Diesel") ) # Nur historische Daten (kein Netzwerkaufruf) statistik_pkw_kraftstoff_zeitreihe(include_current_year = FALSE)

title: Kfz-Daten – Österreich (Neuzulassungen & Bestand)
text: Gibt mehr Detail für das aktuelle Jahr (oder Jahressummen 2024/2025): Aufschlüsselung nach Fahrzeugtyp (PKW, Motorrad, LKW …), Bundesland und Kraftstoffart — oder nach Marke × Fahrzeugtyp. Hinweis: Für eine lange Zeitreihe lieber statistik_pkw_kraftstoff_zeitreihe() verwenden.

title: Kfz-Daten – Österreich (Neuzulassungen & Bestand)
text: Spalten: monat, bundesland, fahrzeugtyp, kraftstoffart, anzahl
code: # Alle Monate des aktuellen Jahres statistik_kfz_neuzulassungen("kraftstoff") # Nur Jänner, Elektro-PKW je Bundesland df <- statistik_kfz_neuzulassungen("kraftstoff", months = "Jänner")  df |>   filter(fahrzeugtyp == "Personenkraftwagen Klasse M1",          kraftstoffart == "Elektro") |>   select(bundesland, anzahl) |>   arrange(desc(anzahl)) # Jahressumme 2025 statistik_kfz_neuzulassungen("kraftstoff", year = 2025)

title: Kfz-Daten – Österreich (Neuzulassungen & Bestand)
text: Spalten: monat, marke, fahrzeugtyp, anzahl
code: dm <- statistik_kfz_neuzulassungen("marken")  # Top 10 PKW-Marken im Jänner dm |>   filter(monat == "Jänner",          grepl("Personenkraftwagen", fahrzeugtyp),          marke != "Kraftfahrzeuge insgesamt") |>   slice_max(anzahl, n = 10) |>   select(marke, anzahl)

title: Kfz-Daten – Österreich (Neuzulassungen & Bestand)
text: Nutzt die OGD REST API (data.statistik.gv.) und gibt monatliche PKW-Neuzulassungen nach Marke von Jänner 2000 bis heute zurück. Spalten: monat, date, brand, producing_country, is_ev_only, marke, anzahl Wichtig: Manche Marken erscheinen mehrfach nach Herstellungsland. "AUDI (D)" und "AUDI (H)" sind getrennte Zeilen — für eine Gesamt-Zahl nach brand gruppieren, nicht nach marke.
code: # Alle Marken im aktuellen Jahr statistik_pkw_marken_zeitreihe(   date_from = paste0(format(Sys.Date(), "%Y"), "-01") ) # Ausgewählte Marken seit 2015 statistik_pkw_marken_zeitreihe(   date_from = "2015-01",   marken    = c("VW", "SKODA", "BMW", "MERCEDES", "TOYOTA", "TESLA") ) # Nur reine E-Auto-Marken seit 2020 statistik_pkw_marken_zeitreihe(date_from = "2020-01") |>   filter(is_ev_only) |>   summarise(anzahl = sum(anzahl, na.rm = TRUE), .by = c(date, brand)) |>   arrange(date, desc(anzahl)) # Top 15 Marken im letzten verfügbaren Monat statistik_pkw_marken_zeitreihe() |>   filter(date == max(date)) |>   summarise(anzahl = sum(anzahl, na.rm = TRUE), .by = brand) |>   slice_max(anzahl, n = 15)

what: Download ACLED events via the myACLED OAuth API — acled_escape_cli_text
title: Download ACLED events via the myACLED OAuth API — acled_escape_cli_text
text: Calls current ACLED API (acleddata.com/api/acled/read) OAuth login. Handles pagination (5,000 rows per page) optional interactive event-type selection. Vault guide: topics/_wiki/methoden/acled-api-journalist.md.

title: Download ACLED events via the myACLED OAuth API — acled_escape_cli_text
code: acled_escape_cli_text(x)

title: Download ACLED events via the myACLED OAuth API — acled_escape_cli_text
text: email_address myACLED email address. NULL missing, uses ACLED_EMAIL env var. password myACLED password. NULL missing, uses ACLED_PASSWORD env var. token Optional Bearer token (24 h). Skips login set. Uses ACLED_ACCESS_TOKEN env var NULL. start_date Character string Date object. Start date (YYYY-MM-DD). Required. end_date Character string Date object. End date (YYYY-MM-DD). Required. country Character vector. Optional. ACLED country name(s). Example: c("Yemen", "Syria"). region Character numeric vector. Optional. ACLED region name(s) code(s). See ACLED API documentation acled.api::get.api.regions(). admin1 Character vector. Optional. First-level administrative region(s). admin2 Character vector. Optional. Second-level administrative region(s). admin3 Character vector. Optional. Third-level administrative region(s). event_types Character vector. Optional. ACLED event type(s). NULL interactive_events = FALSE, event types considered. interactive_events = TRUE, argument ignored initially. Example: c("Battles", "Explosions/Remote violence"). sub_event_types Character vector. Optional. ACLED sub-event type(s). NULL, sub-types selected event_types considered. interactive_events = TRUE, argument ignored initially. Example: c("Air/drone strike", "Armed clash"). interactive_events Logical. TRUE, prompts user select event types sub-event types. Defaults FALSE. page_limit Numeric. Records per API page request. Max 5000. max_pages Numeric. Maximum pages fetch prevent overly long requests. output_format Character. "df" data.frame "raw_json" raw list. ... Additional query parameters pass directly ACLED API named list (e.g., list(terms_of_use="yes", source="Reuters")). added manually constructed URL string. See ACLED API guide available parameters.

title: Download ACLED events via the myACLED OAuth API — acled_escape_cli_text
text: data.frame containing queried ACLED event data (output_format = "df"), list parsed JSON content page (output_format = "raw_json"). Returns NULL critical error occurs data found.

title: Download ACLED events via the myACLED OAuth API — acled_escape_cli_text
text: Authentication uses myACLED email + password (separate API key). Provide via arguments environment variables ACLED_EMAIL ACLED_PASSWORD.

title: Download ACLED events via the myACLED OAuth API — acled_escape_cli_text
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # --- Set environment variables first (recommended) --- # Sys.setenv(ACLED_EMAIL = "you@newsroom.example") # Sys.setenv(ACLED_PASSWORD = "your-myacled-password")  # Lebanon events (Sep 2024 – May 2026) lebanon = acled_download_events(   start_date = "2024-09-01",   end_date = "2026-05-31",   country = "Lebanon" ) if (!is.null(yemen_explosions) && nrow(yemen_explosions) > 0) {   print(head(yemen_explosions[, c("event_date", "country", "admin1", "event_type")])) }  # Example 2: Interactive selection (run in an interactive R session) # interactive_data = acled_download_events( #   start_date = "2024-01-01", #   end_date = "2024-01-07", #   country = "Ukraine", #   interactive_events = TRUE # ) # if (!is.null(interactive_data)) print(head(interactive_data)) } # }

POINT MULTIPOINT treated point markers, LINESTRING MULTILINESTRING line markers, POLYGON MULTIPOLYGON area markers. function automatically transform coordinates EPSG:4326 (WGS 84) different CRS. code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Ensure required packages are installed # install.packages(c("sf", "httr", "jsonlite"))  # --- Example 1: Using a data.frame for Points --- df_points <- data.frame(   name = c("Brandenburg Gate", "Reichstag Building"),   longitude = c(13.3777, 13.3761),   latitude = c(52.5163, 52.5186),   size = c(1.5, 1.0),   info = c("An 18th-century monument.", "Historic government building.") )  # Upload to Datawrapper, mapping columns to properties dw_update_locator_map(   data = df_points,   chart_id = "YOUR_CHART_ID",   lon = "longitude",   lat = "latitude",   title = "name",   tooltip_text = "info",   scale = "size",   markerColor = "#c93e46" )  # --- Example 2: Using an sf object for Polygons (Areas) --- # Create a sample sf polygon object pol1 <- sf::st_polygon(list(rbind(c(0,0), c(1,0), c(1,1), c(0,1), c(0,0)))) pol2 <- sf::st_polygon(list(rbind(c(2,2), c(3,2), c(3,3), c(2,3), c(2,2)))) sf_areas <- sf::st_sf(   name = c("Area 1", "Area 2"),   fill_col = c("#15607acc", "#ff8000cc"),   geometry = sf::st_sfc(pol1, pol2, crs = 4326) )  # Upload to Datawrapper dw_update_locator_map(   data = sf_areas,   chart_id = "YOUR_CHART_ID",   tooltip_text = "name",   fill_color = "fill_col",   stroke_width = 2 ) } # }

code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Get Arctic data for 2022 and 2023 with default 5-day smoothing ice_data_arctic_smooth = fetch_sea_ice_extent(years = c(2022, 2023)) print(head(ice_data_arctic_smooth))  # Get Arctic data for 2022 and 2023 with NO smoothing (raw data) ice_data_arctic_raw = fetch_sea_ice_extent(years = c(2022, 2023), window = 1) print(head(ice_data_arctic_raw))  # Example of plotting (requires ggplot2, dplyr, lubridate) if (requireNamespace("ggplot2", quietly = TRUE) &&     requireNamespace("dplyr", quietly = TRUE) &&     requireNamespace("lubridate", quietly = TRUE)) {    library(ggplot2)   library(dplyr)   library(lubridate)    # Plot 2023 Arctic raw data against historical raw range/median   fetch_sea_ice_extent(years = 2023, pole = "north", window = 1) %>%     filter(variable %in% c("2023", "q0_1981_to_2010", "q100_1981_to_2010", "q50_1981_to_2010")) %>%     # Add a temporary DOY column for plotting alignment     mutate(doy = yday(date)) %>%     ggplot(aes(x = doy, y = ice_extent_mi_sqkm, color = variable)) +     geom_line(linewidth = 1) +     labs(title = "Arctic Sea Ice Extent (2023 Raw vs 1981-2010 Raw)",          x = "Day of Year",          y = "Ice Extent (Million sq km)",          color = "Data Series") +     theme_minimal() } } # }

what: Create a Customized Data Frame Search Function — ff
title: Create a Customized Data Frame Search Function — ff
text: function acts factory, returning another function tailored search within specific data frame (sf object). returned search function can perform case-insensitive regular expression searches fuzzy string matching.

title: Create a Customized Data Frame Search Function — ff
code: ff(df, default_cols = NULL)

title: Create a Customized Data Frame Search Function — ff
text: df data.frame sf object searched. default_cols optional character vector column names used default set searching returned function. NULL (default), columns specified call returned search function, character factor columns df searched. sf objects, geometry column automatically excluded default. specified columns character factor, coerced character searching, warning search function used.

title: Create a Customized Data Frame Search Function — ff
text: new function following signature: function(pattern, fuzzy = FALSE, top_n = 3, fuzzy_method = "osa", cols = NULL) pattern character string (regex plain text fuzzy) search . fuzzy Logical. TRUE, performs fuzzy matching using stringdist package find top_n similar rows. Defaults FALSE (regex search). stringdist package must installed imported package. top_n Integer. fuzzy = TRUE, number best matches return. Defaults 3. fuzzy_method Character. method stringdist calculation fuzzy = TRUE. Defaults "osa" (Optimal String Alignment). See ?stringdist::stringdistmethods options. cols optional character vector column names search within specific call. provided, overrides default_cols set ff called. NULL (default), search use default_cols (provided ff) character/factor columns default_cols set. returned function take pattern search data frame originally passed ff, returning matching rows.

title: Create a Customized Data Frame Search Function — ff
code: # Create a sample data frame my_data = data.frame(   ID = 1:5,   Name = c("Apple Pie", "Banana Bread", "Cherry Cake", "Date Squares", "Elderflower Tea"),   Category = factor(c("Dessert", "Baked Good", "Dessert", "Snack", "Beverage")),   Description = c("Sweet apple filling", "Moist banana loaf",                   "Rich cherry flavor", "Chewy dates", "Refreshing floral drink"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  # 1. Searcher with no default columns (will search all char/factor cols by default) search_all_defaults = ff(my_data) search_all_defaults("apple") # Searches Name, Category, Description #>   ID      Name Category         Description #> 1  1 Apple Pie  Dessert Sweet apple filling search_all_defaults("apple", cols = "Name") # Overrides to search only Name #>   ID      Name Category         Description #> 1  1 Apple Pie  Dessert Sweet apple filling search_all_defaults("Dessert", cols = c("Name", "Category")) # Searches specified #>   ID        Name Category         Description #> 1  1   Apple Pie  Dessert Sweet apple filling #> 3  3 Cherry Cake  Dessert  Rich cherry flavor  # 2. Searcher with default columns specified search_name_default = ff(my_data, default_cols = "Name") search_name_default("apple") # Searches only Name (default) #>   ID      Name Category         Description #> 1  1 Apple Pie  Dessert Sweet apple filling search_name_default("Dessert") # Searches only Name (default), won't find #> [1] ID          Name        Category    Description #> <0 rows> (or 0-length row.names) search_name_default("Dessert", cols = "Category") # Overrides to search Category #>   ID        Name Category         Description #> 1  1   Apple Pie  Dessert Sweet apple filling #> 3  3 Cherry Cake  Dessert  Rich cherry flavor search_name_default("Bread", cols = c("Name", "Description")) # Override #>   ID         Name   Category       Description #> 2  2 Banana Bread Baked Good Moist banana loaf  # 3. Searcher with multiple default columns search_name_cat_default = ff(my_data, default_cols = c("Name", "Category")) search_name_cat_default("apple") # Searches Name and Category #>   ID      Name Category         Description #> 1  1 Apple Pie  Dessert Sweet apple filling search_name_cat_default("Baked") # Finds in Category #>   ID         Name   Category       Description #> 2  2 Banana Bread Baked Good Moist banana loaf search_name_cat_default("filling", cols = "Description") # Overrides #>   ID      Name Category         Description #> 1  1 Apple Pie  Dessert Sweet apple filling  # Fuzzy search with override search_all_defaults("Banna Bred", fuzzy = TRUE, top_n = 1) # Uses default cols #>   ID         Name Category Description #> 4  4 Date Squares    Snack Chewy dates search_all_defaults("Banna Bred", fuzzy = TRUE, top_n = 1, cols="Name") # Uses override #>   ID         Name   Category       Description #> 2  2 Banana Bread Baked Good Moist banana loaf  # Error if default_cols in ff don't exist # try(ff(my_data, default_cols = "NonExistentCol"))  # Error if cols in searcher don't exist # try(search_all_defaults("test", cols = "NonExistentCol"))

title: Find Contrasting Colors for DST Newspaper Ressorts — find_contrast_color_dst
text: Automatically finds optimal contrasting colors DST newspaper ressort colors. Uses predefined newspaper color palette calculates best contrasting options.

title: Find Contrasting Colors for DST Newspaper Ressorts — find_contrast_color_dst
code: find_contrast_color_dst(   ressort = NULL,   contrast_level = "AA",   color_type = "all" )

title: Find Contrasting Colors for DST Newspaper Ressorts — find_contrast_color_dst
text: ressort Character. Name ressort: "apo", "wirt", "sport", "pano", "kultur", "etat", "wiss", "karriere", "zukunft". NULL, returns ressort contrasts. contrast_level Character. Desired contrast level: "AA" (4.5:1), "AAA" (7:1), "high" (10:1+). Default "AA". color_type Character. Type contrasting color: "dark", "light", "complementary", "". Default "".

text: Downloads commodity price data Gaza Humanitarian Data Exchange (HDX), processes tidy format, can translate commodity names German.

title: Download and Process Gaza Commodity Price Data — gaza_commodity_prices

title: Download and Process Gaza Commodity Price Data — gaza_commodity_prices
text: translate_names Logical. TRUE (default), attempts translate commodity names German using polyglotr::google_translate. Requires polyglotr package. FALSE polyglotr available, commodity_name_german NA.

title: Download and Process Gaza Commodity Price Data — gaza_commodity_prices
text: tibble processed commodity price data. Columns include: commodity_name_english: Original commodity name English. commodity_name_german: Translated commodity name German (NA translation /failed). date: date price observation. absolute_price: price commodity.

title: Download and Process Gaza Commodity Price Data — gaza_commodity_prices
text: Gaza Commodity Prices function fetches Excel file "State Palestine - Price basic commodities Gaza" dataset HDX. cleans column names, identifies relevant commodity price columns, pivots data long format. Dates parsed: one special column representing October 2023 data assigned 2023-10-01, others derived Excel serial date numbers present column headers (e.g., "Nov-23" becomes x45231 2023-11-01). Commodity names (English) can translated. output tibble columns: commodity_name_english, commodity_name_german (optional), date, absolute_price.

text: data frame list containing daily casualty data Gaza.

title: Fetches the daily casualty reports for Gaza — gaza_daily_casualties
text: Data Source: four source values daily casualty reports used build time series: mohtel: Gaza's Ministry Health Telegram channel (primary source) gmotel: Gaza's Government Media Office Telegram channel unocha: UN OCHA reports missing: official report available given date primary source Ministry Health. numbers report include can connect directly act war. example: "Ministry Health policy recording direct casualties war, caused missile strikes war-related injuries. deaths indirectly caused war, add list martyrs. example, children die due malnutrition, lack care, mothers gave birth difficult conditions carried poor health, resulting birth underdeveloped infant dies days lacked proper feeding, cases documented recorded martyrs." Extrapolated Fields: Since official numbers always available, extrapolated fields (prefixed "ext_") provided using following methodology: missing field cumulative one official report daily killed injury count, calculate cumulative using daily increment missing field daily increment cumulative counts, subtracted reported cumulative count prior period missing daily count missing sets numbers given reporting period average difference surrounding periods Breakdown Interval: Since October 2023, Gaza officials reporting specific breakdowns people killed semi-regular basis. 2024, GMO reported breakdowns weekly basis, August 2024 began reporting every 2 weeks. January 2025 form reporting reduced every month. Data Fields: daily report contains fields : report_date: Date YYYY-MM-DD format report_source: Source (mohtel, gmotel, unocha, missing) report_period: Hours length reporting period (24, 48, 0) killed: Total killed persons given report date killed_cum: Cumulative number killed persons report date killed_children_cum: Cumulative number children killed killed_women_cum: Cumulative number women killed injured: Injured persons given report date injured_cum: Cumulative number injured persons civdef_killed_cum: Cumulative emergency services killed med_killed_cum: Cumulative medical personnel killed press_killed_cum: Cumulative journalists killed famine_cum: Cumulative adults & children killed starvation aid_seeker_killed_cum: Cumulative people killed seeking aid many fields "ext_" prefixes extrapolated values

title: Fetches data on damaged infrastructure in Gaza — gaza_infrastructure_damaged
text: Data Source: infrastructure damage data, dataset depends Government Media Office (GMO) Gaza. Reports provided roughly every week (2024) unclear frequently government officials updating numbers reports. reason treated estimates, especially given magnitude difficulty non-combatants enter certain regions Gaza Israeli forces present. Data Context: Gaza nearly entirely destroyed. estimates 90% buildings infrastructure targeted way. Updates statistics slowed earlier weekly frequency every month longer. Extrapolated Fields: Since official numbers always available, can useful timeseries dates values, extrapolated fields (prefixed "ext_") provided. recent numbers 2024, fields display latest known value last received report date. earlier reports less consistent, incrementing daily values derived using difference two known reporting periods order consistent ext_ values provided daily datasets. Data Fields: daily report contains fields : report_date: Date YYYY-MM-DD format civic_buildings.destroyed: Cumulative number government buildings destroyed civic_buildings.ext_destroyed: extrapolated educational_buildings.destroyed: Cumulative number educational buildings destroyed educational_buildings.ext_destroyed: extrapolated educational_buildings.damaged: Cumulative number educational buildings partially damaged educational_buildings.ext_damaged: extrapolated places_of_worship.mosques_destroyed: Cumulative number mosques destroyed places_of_worship.ext_mosques_destroyed: extrapolated places_of_worship.mosques_damaged: Cumulative number mosques partially damaged places_of_worship.ext_mosques_damaged: extrapolated places_of_worship.churches_destroyed: Cumulative number churches destroyed places_of_worship.ext_churches_destroyed: extrapolated residential.destroyed: Cumulative number residential units destroyed residential.ext_destroyed: extrapolated Note Mosque Classification: distribution destroyed vs. damaged mosques shifted considerably May. May 18th report, number mosques classified destroyed sharply increased, number classified damaged decreased. unclear whether due reporting difficulties, delays, change classification approach.

title: Fetches the complete list of known individuals killed in Gaza — gaza_killed
text: Data Source Updates: file updated new list released Gaza's Ministry Health. list incorporates following releases Ministry Health: First release (January 5th, 2024): Hospitals reporting South November 2nd 2023 North. Additionally, 21 records included earlier release noted February update. Second release (March 29th, 2024): Included submissions public Ministry (.e., families killed). Changes detailed April 29th update. Third release (April 30th, 2024): Released May 5th Ministry. Changes detailed June 26th update. Fourth release (June 30th, 2024): Released July 24th Ministry. Changes detailed September 7th update. Fifth release (August 31st, 2024): Released around September 15th Ministry. Changes detailed September 21st update. Sixth release (March 23rd, 2025): Released day Ministry via Iraq Body Count. Changes detailed May 11th update. Seventh release (June 15th, 2025): Released June 23rd Ministry via Iraq Body Count. Changes detailed July 6th update. Eighth release (July 15th, 2025): Released July 16th Ministry via Iraq Body Count. Changes detailed July 20th update. Ninth release (July 31st, 2025): Released August 4th Ministry via Iraq Body Count. Changes detailed August 17th update. Data Limitations: initial January 2024 update, Ministry indicated following list: missing persons bodies trapped rubble counted unidentified people arrived hospitals counted unidentified persons whose bodies handed occupation counted buried families without passing hospitals counted victims Gaza North Gaza counted date stopping information system November aggregate numbers Daily Casualties - Gaza dataset necessarily diverge list due number unidentified people. Data Fields: record contains: name: Original Arabic name source list en_name: English name translation id: Unique string identifier (format may change) dob: Date birth YYYY-MM-DD format, empty string available sex: "m" male "f" female age: Age number source: Source indicator - "h" (Ministry Health), "c" (Public Submission), "j" (judicial house committee), "u" (unknown)

Flash Updates occasionally miss days March 25 available West Bank weekly basis. Data Fields: daily report contains fields : report_date: Date YYYY-MM-DD format verified.killed: Killed persons given report date (verified) verified.killed_cum: Cumulative number confirmed killed persons (verified) verified.injured: Injured persons given report date (verified) verified.injured_cum: Cumulative number injured persons (verified) verified.killed_children: Number children killed given report date (verified) verified.killed_children_cum: Cumulative number children killed (verified) verified.injured_children: Number children injured given report date (verified) verified.injured_children_cum: Cumulative number children injured (verified) killed_cum: verified.killed_cum yet independently verified killed_children_cum: verified.killed_children_cum yet independently verified injured_cum: verified.injured_cum yet independently verified injured_children_cum: verified.injured_children_cum yet independently verified settler_attacks_cum: Cumulative number attacks settlers civilians flash_source: Either "un" "fill" (see March 25 update detail)

title: Download SPARTACUS Gridded Climate Data — geosphere_download_spartacus
text: dest_dir Directory save file. resolution Character string specifying temporal resolution. Allowed values: "daily", "monthly", "seasonal", "yearly". year year data (e.g., 2020). variable_type climate variable type (e.g., "TX", "TM", "RR", "SA"). Allowed values depend chosen resolution schema. month Integer, month (1-12). used resolution = "monthly" monthly data stored separate files per month (schema-dependent). Current example schema monthly assumes one file per year. season_code Character string season. used resolution = "seasonal". Example: "DJF", "MAM", "JJA", "SON". (Schema-dependent). base_data_url Base URL Geosphere data. user_agent Custom User-Agent. overwrite Logical, whether overwrite existing files. verbose Logical, verbose output. timeout_seconds Request timeout.

title: Explore Geosphere Resource Metadata and Requirements — geosphere_explore_resource
text: named list element corresponds unique combination type, mode, response_formats found resource_id. name element descriptive string like "station_historical_csv". element list containing: combination list detailing specific type, mode, formats. metadata full raw metadata list fetched API's /metadata endpoint (NULL failed). available_parameters data frame (tibble) available parameters (found metadata). time_coverage list indicating start/end times (found metadata). spatial_info list spatial details (CRS, bbox, etc.) (found metadata). required_query_params character vector listing query parameters identified required probing data endpoint (NULL determination failed). title: Explore Geosphere Resource Metadata and Requirements — geosphere_explore_resource
text: function first retrieves list available datasets using geosphere_get_datasets. filters specified resource_id. unique combination type, mode, format found ID, retrieves descriptive metadata (available parameters, time range, etc.) resource's /metadata endpoint via direct HTTP calls. Finally, combination, makes deliberate (parameter-less) request main data endpoint provoke error message. parsing error message (typically JSON 'detail' field), accurately identifies query parameters API requires specific data endpoint. Requires geosphere_get_datasets function available.

title: Explore Geosphere Resource Metadata and Requirements — geosphere_explore_resource
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Requires geosphere_get_datasets to be available  # Explore a resource known to have multiple combinations exploration_results = geosphere_explore_resource(   resource_id = "apolis_short-v1-1d-100m" )  # The function prints summaries by default. # The returned object is a list named by combinations: print(names(exploration_results)) # > [1] "grid_historical_geojson"      "grid_historical_netcdf" ...  # Inspect the details for one specific combination: if ("grid_historical_geojson" %in% names(exploration_results)) {    # Check the *required* parameters found by probing the data endpoint:    print(exploration_results$grid_historical_geojson$required_query_params)    # Should show: [1] "parameters" "start" "end" "bbox" (based on example error)     # Compare with *available* parameters from metadata:    print(utils::head(exploration_results$grid_historical_geojson$available_parameters)) }  # Explore a resource with likely only one combination exploration_klima = geosphere_explore_resource(resource_id = "klima-v2-1h") if (!is.null(exploration_klima)) {    print(names(exploration_klima))    # Likely "station_historical_csv" or similar    print(exploration_klima[[1]]$required_query_params)    # Should show something like "parameters", "start", "end", "station_ids" } } # }

Defaults "Europe/Paris".

title: Create an HTML Image Comparison Slider — html_create_image_slider
text: image_left_url Character string. URL "left" "" image. (Required) image_right_url Character string. URL "right" "" image. (Required) alt_left_image Character string. Alt text left image accessibility. Defaults "Left image". alt_right_image Character string. Alt text right image accessibility. Defaults "Right image". label_left Optional. Character string. label displayed left image (e.g., ""). label_right Optional. Character string. label displayed right image (e.g., ""). overlay_image_url Optional. Character string. URL overlay image displayed bottom-right corner. alt_overlay_image Character string. Alt text overlay image. Defaults "Overlay image". overlay_position Character string. Position overlay image ("top-left", "top-right", "bottom-left", "bottom-right"). Defaults "bottom-right". overlay_height Character string. valid CSS value overlay image's height (e.g., "150px", "40%", "auto"). Width scaled automatically. Defaults "40%". initial_slider_position_percent Numeric (0-100). Initial position slider divider. Defaults 50 (centered). slide_on_hover Logical. TRUE, slider divider follows mouse cursor hovering component. Defaults TRUE. enable_drag Logical. TRUE, slider can explicitly dragged mouse click touch. Defaults TRUE. container_width Character string. CSS width slider container (e.g., "500px", "100%"). Defaults "100%". Note: actual width constrained max-width: 615px. container_height Character string. CSS height slider container. "auto", height determined 16:9 aspect ratio. recommended use images aspect ratio. Defaults "auto". handle_line_color Character string. CSS color slider's dividing line. Defaults "rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.9)". handle_grip_color Character string. CSS color slider's circular drag grip. Defaults "#ffffff". handle_arrow_color Character string. CSS color arrows inside drag grip. Defaults "#333333". handle_line_width Numeric. Width dividing line pixels. Defaults 2. handle_grip_size Numeric. Diameter circular grip pixels. Defaults 40. label_font_family Character string. CSS font-family labels. Defaults "system-ui, sans-serif". label_font_size Character string. CSS font-size labels. Defaults "0.9em". label_text_color Character string. CSS color label text. Defaults "white". label_background_color Character string. CSS background-color labels. Defaults "rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.6)". label_padding Character string. CSS padding labels (e.g., "5px 10px"). Defaults "5px 10px". label_vertical_position Character string. Vertical position labels ("top" "bottom"). Defaults "bottom". border_radius Character string. CSS border-radius main container. Defaults "8px". show_labels_on_hover Logical. TRUE, labels visible hovering slider. Defaults FALSE (always visible provided). slider_aria_label Character string. ARIA label slider functionality, screen readers. Defaults "Image comparison slider". # — Accessibility Parameters —

code: html_create_image_swiper(   image_data,   overlay_path = NULL,   gradient_color = "hsla(180, 23%, 81%, 1)",   gradient_stops = NULL,   aspect_ratio = "1/1",   max_width = NULL,   debounce_delay = 0,   animation_duration = 0.3,   object_fit = "contain",   main_image_width = 60,   output_file = NULL,   overwrite = TRUE,   dot_size = "0.625rem",   caption_fade_duration = 0.5,   include_html_header = FALSE,   title = NULL,   subtitle = NULL,   caption_position = c("inside", "outside"),   outside_caption_position = c("bottom", "top"),   dots_position = c("bottom", "top"),   full_screen_mobile = FALSE )

title: Create a Self-Contained HTML Image Swiper — html_create_image_swiper
text: image_data list lists. inner list must contain path (string image source URL) can optionally contain caption (string displayed image). overlay_path optional string specifying path overlay image. gradient_color optional string specifying color edge gradient. Defaults "hsla(180, 23%, 81%, 1)" (light blue-green). Set NULL disable gradient. gradient_stops optional named character vector names percentage stops (e.g., '0', '50') values CSS colors (e.g., '#ffffff', 'transparent'). allows creating complex, multi-color gradients. NULL (default), standard gradient created using gradient_color. aspect_ratio string defining aspect ratio slider items, e.g., "1/1" square "16/9" widescreen. Defaults "1/1". max_width optional numeric value maximum width container pixels. Defaults NULL, allowing slider fill parent's width. debounce_delay optional integer. debounce delay milliseconds scroll event. Defaults 0 (delay). Set positive value (e.g., 50) delay dot indicator update scrolling paused. animation_duration optional numeric value. duration dot indicator's grow/shrink animation seconds. Defaults 0.3. object_fit string specifying image resized fit container. Common values "cover", "contain", "fill", "none", "scale-". Defaults "contain". main_image_width optional numeric value (1-100) percentage width central image. Defaults 60. output_file optional string. provided, generated HTML saved file path. Defaults NULL. overwrite logical value. TRUE (default), existing output file overwritten. FALSE, function stop error file already exists. dot_size string specifying CSS size navigation dots (e.g., "1rem", "10px", "4pt"). Defaults "0.625rem". include_html_header logical value. TRUE, output file complete HTML document, including necessary viewport meta tag proper mobile scaling. Defaults FALSE, outputs HTML fragment. title optional string. header title display slider. subtitle optional string. subtitle display title. caption_position string, either "inside" (default) "outside". "inside", captions overlayed image. "outside", placed image standard text. outside_caption_position string, either "bottom" (default) "top". applies caption_position "outside". dots_position string, either "bottom" (default) "top". Controls navigation dots placed relative slider. full_screen_mobile logical value. TRUE, swiper expand full width (margin -20px, width calc(100% + 40px)) screens 615px. Defaults FALSE.

title: Create an HTML Info Card — html_create_info_card
text: main_value Numeric character. primary value display prominently. numeric (e.g., 10000.55), formatted according number_format. character (e.g., "Active"), displayed -. animate_value TRUE main_value numeric, value animated. main_text Character string. short descriptive text displayed main_value (e.g., "Users Online", "Completion Rate"). headline Optional. Character string. headline displayed top card. source_text Optional. Character string. Text source attribution line (e.g., "Source: Annual Report"). source_link Optional. Character string. URL link source_text . source_text must provided. sr_only_main_value_label Optional. Character string. label append main_value specifically screen readers. helps form complete natural sentence. example, main_value "10,000" sr_only_main_value_label "active users", screen reader announce "10,000 active users". NULL, function attempts use main_text fallback label, just main_value main_text also empty. Providing specific label recommended best accessibility. show_percentage_sign Optional. Logical. TRUE, adds percentage sign (%) main value. Defaults FALSE. Use explicitly control show percentage sign. animation_duration_ms Numeric. Duration animations milliseconds. controls number animation (animate_value TRUE) percentage bar animation (show_percentage_bar animate_percentage_bar TRUE). Defaults 1500. animation_trigger_threshold Numeric. Percentage viewport height (0-100) visible bottom triggering animation. example, 5 means animation starts element 5% bottom viewport. Defaults 5. animate_value Logical. TRUE main_value numeric, animates value animation_initial_display final state. Defaults FALSE. animation_initial_display Character string. Initial text main_value animation. used animate_value TRUE. Defaults "0". show_percentage_bar Logical. TRUE main_value number 0 100, displays background bar fills left right. Must TRUE bar animation occur. animate_percentage_bar Logical. TRUE show_percentage_bar TRUE, animates percentage bar 0 final value using animation_duration_ms. Defaults TRUE. percentage_bar_color Character string. CSS color percentage bar. used show_percentage_bar TRUE. Defaults "rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.1)". ressort_name Optional. Character string. name "ressort" (e.g., department, section like 'dst_wirtschaft', 'dst_apo'). provided recognized internal list, applies predefined color palette (background, text, border). Overrides default colors unless specific color arguments also provided. background_color Optional. Character string. CSS color card's background (e.g., "#f0f0f0", "white"). Defaults light gray. Overridden ressort_name explicitly set. text_color Optional. Character string. CSS color card's text (e.g., "#333333", "black"). Defaults dark gray. Overridden ressort_name explicitly set. border_color Optional. Character string. CSS color card's border (e.g., "#cccccc", "blue"). Defaults medium gray. Overridden ressort_name (uses accent color) explicitly set. border_gradient Optional. Character string. CSS gradient card's border. used NULL empty. card_width Optional. Character string. CSS width card (e.g., "300px", "100%", "auto"). Defaults "min(100%, 350px)" ensures card never wider 350px can shrink smaller screens. border_width Optional. Numeric. Width card's border pixels. Defaults 2. font_family Optional. Character string. CSS font-family string card's text. Defaults "STMatilda Text Variable, system-ui, serif". headline_css Optional. Character string. full inline CSS string (e.g. "font-size: 1.1em; font-weight: bold;") headline. main_text_css Optional. Character string. full inline CSS string (e.g. "font-size: 1.1em;") main text. main_value_css Optional. Character string. full inline CSS string (e.g. "font-size: 1.7em; font-weight: bold;") main value. source_css Optional. Character string. full inline CSS string (e.g. "font-size: 0.8em;") source text. shadow_intensity Optional. Character string. Controls box shadow intensity. Valid values: "none", "low", "middle" ("medium"), "intense" ("high"). Defaults "middle". number_format Optional. Character string. locale use number formatting (e.g., "de-DE" German format). NULL, specific formatting applied. Common values: "de-DE" (German), "en-US" (US), "fr-FR" (French). applies animate_value TRUE. # — Formatting Parameters —

what: Create a two-level spatial lookup from a geospatial raster — spatial_create_lookup
title: Create a two-level spatial lookup from a geospatial raster — spatial_create_lookup
text: function processes geospatial raster file (e.g., GeoTIFF NetCDF) generate two-level lookup structure suitable web applications. partitions raster cells specified number spatially coherent groups using k-means clustering exports data series CSV files. output consists : main index file (main.csv) containing overall bounding box group. directory individual group files (g_*.csv), file contains detailed coordinates bounding box every cell within group.

title: Create a two-level spatial lookup from a geospatial raster — spatial_create_lookup
code: spatial_create_lookup(   input_raster_file,   output_base_dir,   n_groups = 500,   crs_output = "EPSG:4326",   seed = 123,   resampling_method = "bilinear" )

title: Create a two-level spatial lookup from a geospatial raster — spatial_create_lookup
text: input_raster_file string path input raster file. Must format readable terra::rast(). output_base_dir string path directory output lookups folder created. n_groups target number groups (clusters) partition raster cells . determines number rows main.csv. crs_output target Coordinate Reference System (CRS) output data, specified EPSG string (e.g., "EPSG:4326"). Defaults WGS 84, standard web maps. seed integer set random seed k-means clustering, ensuring reproducible results. Defaults 123. resampling_method resampling method reprojection. Defaults "bilinear". Use "near" categorical data. See terra::project.

title: Create a two-level spatial lookup from a geospatial raster — spatial_create_lookup
text: Invisibly returns list containing paths generated main.csv file, directory group CSVs, path grid vector file, path reprojected raster file.

title: Get Directory of the Currently Executing R Script — sys_get_script_dir
text: function attempts robustly determine return absolute path directory containing currently executing R script. tries various methods accommodate different execution contexts RStudio, knitr (R Markdown/Quarto), source(), Rscript. prints informative message indicating method used.

title: Get Directory of the Currently Executing R Script — sys_get_script_dir

title: Get Directory of the Currently Executing R Script — sys_get_script_dir

TRUE (default), prints message indicating script directory determined failed.

title: Get Directory of the Currently Executing R Script — sys_get_script_dir
text: character string representing absolute path directory currently executing script.

title: Get Directory of the Currently Executing R Script — sys_get_script_dir
text: function employs following strategies order find script's path: RStudio API: run within RStudio document active, uses rstudioapi::getActiveDocumentContext()$path. knitr Environment: script processed knitr (e.g., within R Markdown Quarto document), uses knitr::current_input(dir = FALSE) get source file, directory. source() Context: script source()d, checks parent frames ofile component. Rscript Execution: script run via Rscript command line, parses commandArgs() --file= argument. methods fail determine script's path, function stop error message. fall back working directory (getwd()) avoid ambiguity. returned path normalized absolute path.

title: Get Directory of the Currently Executing R Script — sys_get_script_dir

When run directly in RStudio (after saving the script): #    script_dir <- sys_get_script_dir() #    # Expected output: #    # ✓ Script directory determined via User Override (File Path): /path/to/temp_dir_for_file #    # or for auto-detection: #    # ℹ Script directory determined via RStudio API: /path/to/your/script #    print(script_dir)  # 5. Using the override (e.g., for testing or known path): temp_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".R") writeLines("print('hello')", temp_file) known_dir <- sys_get_script_dir(script_path_override = temp_file) # verbose=TRUE by default unlink(temp_file)  temp_dir <- tempfile("my_dir_") dir.create(temp_dir) known_dir_from_dir <- sys_get_script_dir(script_path_override = temp_dir) unlink(temp_dir, recursive = TRUE)  # Example of failure: tryCatch({   # In a context where it can't be found (e.g. bare R console with no script)   sys_get_script_dir(verbose = TRUE) }, error = function(e) { message(e$message) }) } # }

title: Fetch casualty data from UN OCHA's Protection of Civilians database — un_ocha_fatalities
text: Data source: underlying data comes OCHA's Protection Civilians (POC) database. Incidents independently verified OCHA field staff require least two independent, reliable sources entry. Coverage note: Casualties Gaza hostilities began 7 October 2023 included ; figures appear OCHA's Humanitarian Situation Updates. Implementation notes: Data retrieved replicating POST requests Power BI backend API (wabi-north-europe-j-primary-api.analysis.windows.net).  four dashboards requires distinct X-PowerBI-ResourceKey header. Dataset-specific notes: pal_fatalities — grouped governorate (note: "Govornorate" actual spelling OCHA's data model, typo ). null guard Hostilities always applied; omitting returns different totals. isr_fatalities — uses CountNonNull aggregation Fatalities. Group "Region" (Area) returns totals Gaza Strip, West Bank, Israel. Also supports grouping "Date", "SA (groups)", "Govornorate (groups)", filtering "victim_affiliation (groups) 2" (e.g., "Israeli Civilians", "Security forces", "Civilian-settler"). pal_injuries — Poc_Period ('yes', 'Yes') filter always applied; omitting returns different total. date column view "Date:" (trailing colon — real data model quirk). Sanity-check total: 165,765. isr_injuries — grouped Community. Uses Window-based pagination (1,000 rows/page RestartToken). Uses CountNonNull. measure column name contains trailing space ("Injuries ") — real data model name. Sanity-check total: 6,697.
